summary: Soundboard capture of a solid ’85 show featuring a strong Jack Straw opener, Peggy‑O and a jazzy Never Trust a Woman/Good Time Blues jam.
review: The recording is a decent soundboard tape with good sonic timbre, though a few listeners note occasional looseness in the handling. The performance is typical of a 1985 Dead show – upbeat, loud, and full of improvisational moments, highlighted by Jack Straw, Peggy‑O and Brent’s adventurous keyboard work on Never Trust a Woman/Good Time Blues.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers describe an enjoyable but not exceptional show with a generally good board recording; one strong negative and several middling scores keep the overall rating moderate.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Nice ‘85 board sound with clear timbre; occasional slip/looseness noted but no major drop‑outs or muffling.

standout_songs: Never Trust a Woman / Good Time Blues
show_quality.setlist_flow: The set opens strongly with Jack Straw, maintains high energy through Peggy‑O, and features a characteristic mid‑set jam where Brent’s keyboards take the lead; overall a typical, lively ’85 performance.

band_member_comments.Keys: Brent's keyboards delivered a jazzy improvisational jam on Never Trust a Woman/Good Time Blues.
